paper_title: Chemometric Analysis of the Relationship among Physicochemical Variables

methods.description: Analysis of the correlation matrix to identify significant correlations among physicochemical variables.

steps.description: The analysis of the correlation matrix revealed significant correlations among certain variables.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation matrix for significant correlations

steps.description: Physicochemical data were analyzed by estimating the Spearman correlation coefficient for each pairwise comparison, which is displayed in each cell.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize Spearman correlation coefficients in matrix

methods.description: FAMD analysis to explain variance among physicochemical parameters and identify significant contributors to components.

steps.description: The FAMD analysis showed that components 1, 2, and 3, including all physicochemical parameters, explained 81.9% of the cumulative variance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform FAMD to explain variance among parameters

steps.description: Salinity, conductivity, K, ash content, total acidity, pH, Ca, Na, HMF, free acidity, °Brix, TDS, and resistivity contributed significantly (p < 0.001 for all) to component 1.
steps.searchable_summary: identify significant contributors to FAMD components

methods.description: HCPC analysis to associate physicochemical parameters with harvest periods and identify clusters.

steps.description: The HCPC analysis further supported the association between the physicochemical parameters and the harvest period during the dry season.
steps.searchable_summary: perform HCPC to associate parameters with harvest periods

steps.description: Clusters 1, 2, and 3 were predominantly associated with the harvest periods of February, March, and April, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: identify clusters associated with harvest periods

methods.description: Analysis of pollen species composition to identify patterns and temporal shifts in dominant pollen sources.

steps.description: The hierarchical cluster analysis revealed distinct patterns in the pollen species compositions among the honey samples harvested in February, March, and April.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen species composition for patterns

steps.description: The analysis demonstrated that the pollen species composition of the samples from March and April displayed greater similarity compared to the samples from February.
steps.searchable_summary: identify temporal shifts in pollen species composition
